title: Subscribe / Follow
surface: RSS + LinkedIn, no email — preserves v1 posture
summary: One-surface page for follow options: RSS, full-content RSS, LinkedIn link. No email capture. Preserves v1's intentional no-marketing-tells posture.

strategy.decision: Inherit v1's no-email-capture rule unchanged. Add a dedicated Subscribe page (vs v1's footer-link-only treatment) so the affordance is discoverable without breaking the no-marketing-tells rule. Show RSS URL + full-content RSS URL + LinkedIn-follow URL on one page, each with a one-line explanation.
strategy.why: Hiring-evaluator persona's drop-pattern: marketing tells (Subscribe! popups, email gates, trending widgets) read as personal-brand performance. v1 explicitly chose to not capture emails. v2 inherits that. The only change is making the existing RSS affordance discoverable as a surface, not just a footer link.
strategy.shipped: v1: RSS surfaces in footer (/blog/rss.xml and /blog/full-content-rss.xml), in 'More' nav menu, and as link in /llms.txt (now fixed in 628d93f). No on-site subscribe affordance beyond those links.
strategy.gap: v2 Subscribe-Follow surface gives the affordance a page-level home: explainer copy + RSS URL + full-content RSS URL + LinkedIn-follow link. Discoverable from primary nav (or About) without being a popup or modal interruption mid-post.
strategy.question: None blocking. Optional consideration: should the Subscribe-Follow surface explain *what the cadence is* per D4 (so readers know what they're subscribing to)?

breaksFromV1: dedicated Subscribe surface with explainer (vs footer-link-only)

currentState.summary: v1: subscribe affordances exist (RSS URLs in footer) but no dedicated surface. Reader has to look for them.
currentState.annotation: v2 adds the surface without violating no-marketing-tells. Same affordances, more discoverable.
